page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the x86_64 architecture. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs derived from 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor operating at 2.3 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512) and Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), which accelerate vector and matrix operations. The instance is configured with 192 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 82682.91 and a PassMark CPU score of 82913.7. Single-core performance is moderate to strong, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2238.0 and a stress-ng single-core score of 2042.08. The system demonstrates top-tier capabilities in specific workloads, achieving 105.66 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, 129.97 GB/sec in static web serving throughput, and 32707.07 in PassMark database operations. Large-block memory bandwidth reaches 298.66 GB/sec for read operations and 127.91 GB/sec for write operations.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highcpu-96 offers high compute density but presents a clear tradeoff in memory capacity, providing only 2.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This makes it less suitable for memory-intensive databases or massive in-memory datasets. The lack of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPUs means all storage must be network-attached, and GPU-accelerated workloads are unsupported. However, for compute-heavy tasks that do not require massive memory footprints, the hardware profile delivers high CPU-to-RAM cost efficiency by avoiding the premium associated with larger memory allocations.

This instance is suited for compute-bound workloads such as software compilation, text and image processing, web serving, and database operations. It is also capable of handling CPU-based machine learning tasks, as demonstrated by its strong performance in LLM inference benchmarks, achieving 294.08 tokens per second for prompt processing on a medium 7B model.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server configured with 96 dedicated vCPUs from 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It features 192 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio, with no local storage or GPUs. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions. Benchmarks show strong multi-core capabilities, with a PassMark CPU score of 82913.7 and top-tier performance in database operations and static web serving. While the low memory-to-vCPU ratio and lack of local storage present tradeoffs for memory-heavy or disk-intensive tasks, the instance offers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-dense workloads. It is suited for software compilation, web serving, text processing, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering strong multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highcpu-96 features 96 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized x86_64 platform featuring ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and advanced matrix extensions for demanding parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 96 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz.
Equipped with 192 GB of RAM, providing a compute-focused 2.0 GB per vCPU ratio.
Supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions for accelerated vector and matrix math.
Delivers strong multi-core performance and top-tier static web serving throughput.
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and CPU-based LLM inference.
Lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage.
